what_it_does: A design-system specimen page that visually enumerates the dimensional tokens: the density-aware spacing scale (--d-1..--d-12), the radius scale (--r-xs..--r-2xl), and the elevation/shadow ramp (--shadow-xs..--shadow-modal plus --elev-2). Links ../styles.css and uses a small inline script to generate a labelled bar per spacing token, a rounded box per radius token, and a shadowed box per elevation token.
observations: Carries the @dsCard group="Spacing" comment header with name/subtitle/viewport metadata for the gallery harness.

Spacing rows are generated from a JS array mapping token names to nominal px ([--d-1,4]...[--d-12,48]); each bar's width is set via var(<token>,0) so the actual rendered width comes from the live token.
Radius demo iterates --r-xs/--r-sm/--r-md/--r-lg/--r-xl/--r-2xl, applying each as border-radius on a box.
Elevation demo iterates --shadow-xs/--shadow-sm/--shadow-card/--shadow-pop/--shadow-modal/--elev-2, applying each as box-shadow.
Subtitle states spacing is density-aware (--d-* scale with data-density) while radii and elevation are fixed ramps.
Uses color-mix(in oklch, var(--pig-content) 6%, var(--zone-ground)) for the radius box fill — references the pigment/zone token model.
The px numbers in the JS array are display-nominal only and unused for sizing (the [v] destructure ignores the second element).
standouts: Bar widths are driven by var(--d-*) directly, so the specimen reflows live with data-density rather than showing static px.
Uses oklch color-mix against pigment/zone tokens for the swatch fills, tying even the demo chrome to the token model.
conventions: Follows the specimen pattern: @dsCard header + self-contained HTML linking the shared stylesheet, content built by a short inline script.
Section headers reuse the uppercase-bronze-label + rotated-gold-gem ::before treatment seen in sibling specimens.
References tokens via var() throughout; mixes pigment via color-mix rather than a literal fill.
concerns: The nominal px values in the spacing array (e.g. '4','8') are carried but unused; if the real --d-* tokens diverge from these labels the array comment value could mislead a reader (the bars themselves stay correct since they read the token).
notes: A specimen that draws the spacing/radius/elevation tokens straight from the stylesheet so they can be eyeballed and verified; spacing bars are density-reactive while radius/elevation are fixed ramps.
questions: Do the named tokens (--d-*, --r-*, --shadow-*, --elev-2, --pig-content, --zone-ground) all exist in styles.css so every row renders, and are the array's nominal px still accurate to the tokens?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the subtitle promises the dimensional tokens (density spacing, radius scale, elevation ramp) and the page renders exactly those three families.
apparent_themes: Fixed ramps for radius and depth
intention_signals: Trying to make the dimensional token system legible and checkable at a glance — proving that spacing responds to density and that radius/elevation form coherent ramps.
novelty: Driving the spacing bars from the live --d-* tokens (so they react to data-density) rather than drawing static px gives the specimen a self-verifying quality.
contribution: Contributes a renderable reference/smoke-test of the spacing, radius, and elevation token families bound to the live stylesheet, showing the density-reactivity of spacing distinctly from the fixed radius/elevation ramps.
summary_for_embedding: A ConceptV design-system specimen HTML page enumerating the density-aware spacing scale (--d-*), the radius scale (--r-*), and the elevation/shadow ramp, each rendered live from the shared CSS tokens.
intention_for_embedding: To visually verify the dimensional tokens — that spacing reacts to density and radius/elevation form coherent fixed ramps — straight from the live stylesheet.
